choice: Encrypt the token + token-secret at rest (app-level envelope encryption with a key outside the database, e.g. a KMS/secret-store key), scoped to the household partition (c49e8550), and never expose them to the client. Since they die daily anyway, on logout/disconnect just drop them and require a fresh handshake; no long-lived secret accumulates. Treat 'Reconnect E*TRADE' (b45b7691) as the universal recovery path for any missing/invalid token.
rationale: These are read-only but still brokerage-account credentials, so plaintext-at-rest is an unacceptable blast radius in a multi-household DB even with row-level isolation. Their built-in daily expiry actually limits exposure, so the main job is encryption-at-rest + household scoping rather than elaborate rotation. This rides on existing isolation (c49e8550) and the reconnect prompt (b45b7691) without new machinery.
